arXiv:1803.04579v1  [cs.LG]  12 Mar 2018

It was the training data pruning too!

f74deb88-cf75-4dd1-8bef-976bc2f497c1.label: text_chunk
f74deb88-cf75-4dd1-8bef-976bc2f497c1.text:
Pramod Kaushik Mudrakarta 1 , Ankur Taly 2 , Mukund Sundararajan 2 , and Kedar Dhamdhere 2 1 The University of Chicago, 2 Google Research pramodkm@uchicago.edu , { ataly, mukunds, kedar } @google.com

Abstract

We study the current best model ( Krishnamurthy et al.

b52f61ef-339c-415a-adba-27ad1a87a401.label: text_chunk
b52f61ef-339c-415a-adba-27ad1a87a401.text: , 2017 ) (KDG) for question answering on tabular data evaluated over the W IKI T ABLE Q UESTIONS dataset. Previous ablation studies performed against this model attributed the model’s performance to certain aspects of its architecture. In this paper, we find that the model’s performance also crucially depends on a certain pruning of the data used to train the model.

328de16c-f223-464e-812c-820cbefd65e4.label: text_chunk
328de16c-f223-464e-812c-820cbefd65e4.text:
Disabling the pruning step drops the accuracy of the model from 43.3% to 36.3%. The large impact on the performance of the KDG model suggests that the pruning may be a useful pre-processing step in training other semantic parsers as well.

1 Introduction

Question answering on tabular data is an impor- tant problem in natural language processing.

9b02d5fb-2008-4d55-a837-3b73d711c72e.label: text_chunk
9b02d5fb-2008-4d55-a837-3b73d711c72e.text:
Re- cently, a number of systems have been proposed for solving the problem using the W IKI T ABLE - Q UESTIONS dataset ( Pasupat and Liang , 2015 ) (henceforth called WTQ).

 This dataset consists of triples of the form h question, table, answer i where the tables are scraped from Wikipedia and ques- tions and answers are gathered via crowdsourcing.

fe5ce806-8cbc-47eb-b546-eacdaa74aca9.label: text_chunk
fe5ce806-8cbc-47eb-b546-eacdaa74aca9.text:
The dataset is quite challenging, with the current best model ( Krishnamurthy et al. , 2017 ) (hence- forth called KDG) achieving a single model 1 ac- curacy of only 43.3% 2 . This is nonetheless a significant improvement compared to the 34.

8% accuracy achieved by the previous best single model ( Haug et al.

28801d9b-84e9-4bc0-8538-e63da0315d12.label: text_chunk
28801d9b-84e9-4bc0-8538-e63da0315d12.text: , 2017 ).We sought to analyze the source of the improve- ment achieved by the KDG model. The KDG pa- per claims that the improvement stems from cer- tain aspects of the model architecture.In this paper, we find that a large part of the im- provement also stems from a certain pruning of the data used to train the model.

168e4d8a-3b4c-4619-9602-f77395a1db55.label: text_chunk
168e4d8a-3b4c-4619-9602-f77395a1db55.text: The KDG sys- tem generates its training data using an algorithm proposed by Pasupat and Liang ( 2016 ). This al- gorithm applies a pruning step (discussed in Sec- tion 2.1 ) to eliminate spurious training data en- tries. We find that without this pruning of the training data, accuracy of the KDG model drops to 36.3%.

19637bff-a28c-4c1f-b572-30b226572c8b.label: text_chunk
19637bff-a28c-4c1f-b572-30b226572c8b.text: We consider this an important finding as the pruning step not only accounts for a large fraction of the improvement in the state-of-the-art KDG model but may also be relevant to training other models. In what follows, we briefly discuss the pruning algorithm, how we identified its im- portance for the KDG model, and its relevance to further work.

96a9c236-bf2c-47f6-b968-a6932f6c4a04.label: text_chunk
96a9c236-bf2c-47f6-b968-a6932f6c4a04.text:
2 KDG Training Data

The KDG system operates by translating a natural language question and a table to a logical form in Lambda-DCS ( Liang , 2013 ). A logical form is an executable formal expression capturing the ques- tion’s meaning. It is executed on the table to ob- tain the final answer.The translation to logical forms is carried out by a deep neural network, also called a neural se- mantic parser .

2a803886-5d64-4de1-b36b-f664b1d5dfec.label: text_chunk
2a803886-5d64-4de1-b36b-f664b1d5dfec.text:
Training the network requires a dataset of questions mapped to one or more log- ical forms. The WTQ dataset only contains the correct answer label for question-table instances. To obtain the desired training data, the KDG system enumerates consistent logical form candi- dates for each h q, t, a i triple in the WTQ dataset, i.e., it enumerates all logical forms that lead to
1 A 5-model ensemble achieves a higher accuracy of 45.

c4197e78-83c4-4835-bd93-390518d814a1.label: text_chunk
c4197e78-83c4-4835-bd93-390518d814a1.text:
9%. We choose to work with a single model as it makes it easier for us to perform our analysis.2 All quoted accuracy numbers are based on our training of the KDG model using the configuration and instructions provided at: https://github.com/allenai/pnp/ tree/wikitables2/experiments/wikitables
the correct answer a on the given table t .

9e0dcfa3-e326-4d4e-8695-416632bba12b.label: text_chunk
9e0dcfa3-e326-4d4e-8695-416632bba12b.text:
For this, it relies on the dynamic programming algo- rithm of Pasupat and Liang ( 2016 ). This algo- rithm is called dynamic programming on denota- tions (DPD).

2.1 Pruning algorithm

A key challenge in generating consistent logical forms is that many of them are spurious , i.e., they do not represent the question’s meaning.

77b25b61-2012-4def-9640-02bfa4e925e7.label: text_chunk
77b25b61-2012-4def-9640-02bfa4e925e7.text: For in- stance, a spurious logical form for the question “which country won the highest number of gold medals” would be one which simply selects the country in the first row of the table. This logi- cal form leads to the correct answer only because countries in the table happen to be sorted in de- scending order.

78e95960-405d-427e-8a65-842686253a75.label: text_chunk
78e95960-405d-427e-8a65-842686253a75.text: Pasupat and Liang ( 2016 ) propose a separate al- gorithm for pruning out spurious logical forms using fictitious tables. Specifically, for each question-table instance in the dataset, fictitious ta- bles are generated 3 , and answers are crowdsourced on them. A logical form that fails to obtain the cor- rect answer on any fictitious table is filtered out.

22fa50b9-b1e8-464a-89f5-6b87ef6b06e4.label: text_chunk
22fa50b9-b1e8-464a-89f5-6b87ef6b06e4.text:
The paper presents an analysis over 300 questions revealing that the algorithm eliminated 92.

1% of the spurious logical forms.

2.2 Importance of pruning for KDG

The KDG system relies on the DPD algorithm for generating consistent logical form candidates for training. It does not explicitly prescribe pruning out spurious logical forms candidates before train- ing.

f7b63311-7501-4716-981f-a894c62b918b.label: text_chunk
f7b63311-7501-4716-981f-a894c62b918b.text:
Since this training set contains spurious log- ical forms, we expected the model to also some- times predict spurious logical forms.However, we were somewhat surprised to find that the logical forms predicted by the KDG model were largely non-spurious.

 We then examined the logical form candidates 4 that the KDG model was trained on.

7194296f-543f-4ded-b290-51e30e38bf1c.label: text_chunk
7194296f-543f-4ded-b290-51e30e38bf1c.text:
Through personal communication with Panupong Pasupat, we learned that all of these candidates had been pruned using the algorithm mentioned in Section 2.1 .We trained the KDG model on unpruned logi- cal form candidates 5 generated using the DPD al-
gorithm, and found its accuracy to drop to 36.3% (from 43.

3%); all configuring parameters were left unchanged 6 .

1b64b9b6-8f23-46bc-8439-1d996c9c8e09.label: text_chunk
1b64b9b6-8f23-46bc-8439-1d996c9c8e09.text:
This implies that pruning out spu- rious logical forms before training is necessary for the performance improvement achieved by the KDG model.

2.3 Directions for further work

Pasupat and Liang ( 2016 ) claimed “the pruned set of logical forms would provide a stronger supervi- sion signal for training a semantic parser”. This paper provides empirical evidence in support of this claim.

e26a6126-bed8-4bba-b740-613f441c2c37.label: text_chunk
e26a6126-bed8-4bba-b740-613f441c2c37.text:
We further believe that the pruning algorithm may also be valuable to models that score logical forms. Such scoring models are typ- ically used by grammar-based semantic parsers such as the one in ( Pasupat and Liang , 2015 ).

 Us- ing the pruning algorithm, the scoring model can be trained to down-score spurious logical forms.

00e944c1-6013-4d47-8853-6a9883ed6e38.label: text_chunk
00e944c1-6013-4d47-8853-6a9883ed6e38.text: Similarly, neural semantic parsers trained using reinforcement learning may use the pruning algo- rithm to only assign rewards to non-spurious logi- cal forms.The original WTQ dataset may also be extended with the fictitious tables used by the pruning al- gorithm.

6b539f52-c8bc-4bc6-8e54-561f9ba42c37.label: text_chunk
6b539f52-c8bc-4bc6-8e54-561f9ba42c37.text: This means that for each h q, t, a i triple in the original dataset, we would add additional triples h q, t 1 , a 1 i , h q, t 2 , a 2 i , . . . where t 1 , t 2 , . . . are the fictitious tables and a 1 , a 2 . . .

a6e6db54-a682-4363-9656-14ee8496cbde.label: text_chunk
a6e6db54-a682-4363-9656-14ee8496cbde.text:
are the cor- responding answers to the question q on those ta- bles.

 Such training data augmentation may im- prove the performance of neural networks that are directly trained over the WTQ dataset, such as ( Neelakantan et al. , 2017 ). The presence of ficti- tious tables in the training set may help these net- works to generalize better, especially on tables that are outside the original WTQ training set.

a78b1932-f3df-4293-8ad6-61b525d01be8.label: text_chunk
a78b1932-f3df-4293-8ad6-61b525d01be8.text:
3 Discussion

Krishnamurthy et al. ( 2017 ) present several abla- tion studies to identify the sources of the perfor- mance improvement achieved by the KDG model. These studies comprehensively cover novel as- pects of the model architecture. On the training side, the studies only vary the number of logical forms per question in the training dataset. Pruning

bd7e8ecd-46b4-4487-ba34-9c2d4f018057.label: text_chunk
bd7e8ecd-46b4-4487-ba34-9c2d4f018057.text:
3 Fictitious tables are generated by applying perturbations, such as shuffling columns, to the original table. We refer to ( Pasupat and Liang , 2016 ) for more details. 4 Available at: https://cs.stanford. edu/˜ppasupat/research/h-strict-all- matching-lfs.tar.gz 5
Available at: https://nlp.stanford.edu/

60ad9ba9-4bd6-431b-a8f1-1133929be822.text:
software/sempre/wikitable/dpd/h-strict- dump-combined.tar.gz
6 It is possible that the model accuracy may slightly in- crease with some hyper-parameter tuning but we do not ex- pect it to wipe out the large drop resulting from disabling the pruning.of the logical forms was not considered. This may have happened inadvertently as the KDG system may have downloaded the logical forms dataset made available by Pasupat et al.

493e0c93-677e-4ea6-a439-65441ef0c184.label: text_chunk
493e0c93-677e-4ea6-a439-65441ef0c184.text: without noticing that it had been pruned out 7 .We note that our finding implies that pruning out spurious logical forms before training is an important factor in the performance improvement achieved by the KDG model. It does not imply that pruning is the only important factor. The ar- chitectural innovations are essential for the perfor- mance improvement too.

28d84f76-e17a-43f2-bf3b-a433fe3aef1d.label: text_chunk
28d84f76-e17a-43f2-bf3b-a433fe3aef1d.text: In light of our finding, we would like to em- phasize that the performance of a machine learn- ing system depends on several factors such as the model architecture, training algorithm, in- put pre-processing, hyper-parameter settings, etc. As Kibler and Langley ( 1988 ) point out, attribut- ing improvements in performance to the individual factors is a valuable exercise in understanding the system, and generating ideas for improving it and other systems.

b8fc5786-bf42-4efe-9815-aead06d2a8ee.label: text_chunk
b8fc5786-bf42-4efe-9815-aead06d2a8ee.text:
In performing these attributions, it is important to consider all factors that may be rel- evant to the system’s performance.

We would like to thank Panupong Pasupat for helpful discussions on the pruning algorithm, and for providing us with the unpruned logical form candidates. We would like to thank Pradeep Dasigi for helping us train the KDG model.

**Summary of Relationships Involving Aristotle**

1. **Historical Context**: Aristotle lived during a significant historical period, referred to as his 'era', and he passed away in 322 BC. His life and work were primarily based in Greece, where he conducted much of his philosophical teaching.

2. **Philosophical Contributions**: Aristotle is a foundational figure in Ancient philosophy, contributing extensively to various disciplines. He founded the Peripatetic school, named after the covered walkways where he taught, and is recognized as the founder of Aristotelianism, which is based on his philosophical teachings.

3. **Areas of Interest**: His main interests included biology, where he made significant contributions to the classification of living organisms, and government, where he explored the structure and function of governing bodies.

4. **Logical Innovations**: Aristotle introduced the concept of syllogism, a method of logical reasoning that is central to his logical theories. He is also known for his notable idea of Hylomorphism, which plays a crucial role in his metaphysical framework.

5. **Influences and Mentorship**: Aristotle's philosophical inquiries were shaped by earlier thinkers such as Heraclitus and Democritus, whose ideas on change and atomism influenced his own theories. Additionally, he was a student of Plato at the Academy in Athens, which significantly impacted his philosophical development.

Overall, Aristotle's relationships highlight his pivotal role in the development of Western philosophy, his diverse interests, and the influences that shaped his thought.
